symptoms.symptom_name: New-onset unilateral temporal headache in a patient over 50, unrelieved by paracetamol

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New temporal headache after age 50 is the sentinel symptom of GCA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Scalp tenderness (burning scalp on brushing hair or lying on pillow)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Superficial temporal/scalp artery inflammation; a recognised cranial GCA feature.

symptoms.symptom_name: Jaw claudication — jaw-muscle pain/fatigue on chewing, relieved by rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Jaw claudication is the most specific clinical feature of GCA and predicts higher ischaemic/ocular risk.
Ischaemic claudication relieved by rest differs from mechanical TMJ joint pain/clicking.

symptoms.symptom_name: Systemic inflammatory symptoms — fatigue, subjective fever/sweats, unintentional weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional symptoms reflect the systemic inflammatory nature of large vessel vasculitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Proximal shoulder-girdle stiffness and ache (polymyalgia rheumatica overlap)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PMR overlaps with GCA in up to half of cases and strengthens the pattern.
Proximal girdle stiffness is the defining feature of PMR.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of visual symptoms (no blurring, diplopia, transient/permanent visual loss, or curtain) — screened negative

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Currently absent visual involvement defines the same-day treatment window; it does not reduce urgency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hardened, prominent, nodular, exquisitely tender right temporal artery with diminished pulse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An abnormal (hard, tender, pulseless) temporal artery on palpation is a strongly supportive clinician-only sign — cannot be obtained via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact visual acuity and fields, no relative afferent pupillary defect on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Formal eye examination is clinician-only; documents that ischaemic optic neuropathy has not yet occurred.

symptoms.symptom_name: Markedly elevated ESR and CRP (anticipated on urgent bloods)

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Loss of supporting evidence for GCA and of a baseline to monitor steroid response — but bloods must not delay treatment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Very high ESR/CRP supports active large vessel vasculitis; requires venepuncture, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Temporal artery biopsy showing granulomatous arteritis (confirmatory)

investigation_details.expected_result: granulomatous transmural inflammation with giant cells (or halo sign on ultrasound)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis remains presumptive; biopsy should be arranged within days but must not delay same-day steroids.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Biopsy/imaging confirms the diagnosis but is a specialist investigation, never available in telehealth in real time.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: New temporal headache + scalp tenderness + jaw claudication + systemic symptoms in a patient over 50 is a highly specific cluster mandating same-day treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent ESR and CRP (with full blood count)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports the diagnosis and provides a baseline for monitoring — arrange same-day/urgently but do NOT delay glucocorticoids waiting for the result.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Temporal artery biopsy or temporal/axillary artery ultrasound
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms GCA; arrange within days via specialist — biopsy remains informative for up to ~2 weeks after starting steroids.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal ophthalmology assessment (visual acuity, fields, fundoscopy)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baseline eye assessment and to detect subclinical ischaemic optic neuropathy; immediate if any visual symptom develops.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT brain / routine neuroimaging as first step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not the diagnostic pathway for GCA and must not divert from same-day steroids and inflammatory markers; the risk here is under-action, not under-imaging.
